# Sliding Windows Analysis Results
```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: true

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from IPython.display import Markdown, display
from src.utils import dashboard_func
from src.utils import analysis as analysis_utils
from src.utils import db
import report_utils.map_utils as map_utils
from report_utils.render_guard import render_guard

MAP_FIG_SIZE = (16, 12)
STUDY_AREA_GDF = None
TOP_CORRIDOR_DICT = {}
SLIDING_WINDOW_RESULTS = None
_SWA_RAN = False

with render_guard("Sliding window results are not available for this study."):
    STUDY_AREA_GDF = map_utils.get_study_area_gdf(study_id)
    _un, _sn = db.get_user_and_study_names(study_id)
    if not db.table_exists(f"swa_short_windows_{_un}_{_sn}", "outputs"):
        display(
            Markdown(
                "_The Sliding Windows Analysis has not been run for this study. "
                "Run the analysis from the Run Analysis tab to include this section._"
            )
        )
    else:
        for mode in ["ped", "bike", "mv"]:
            TOP_CORRIDOR_DICT[mode] = dashboard_func.get_top_corridors_gdf(
                study_id, mode
            )

        analysis_params = analysis_utils.get_analysis_params(study_id)
        top_corridor_limit = analysis_params["current"].get("top_streets_threshold")
        if top_corridor_limit in (None, 0):
            top_corridor_limit = analysis_params["default"].get(
                "top_streets_threshold", 10
            )
        top_corridor_limit = int(top_corridor_limit)

        sliding_window_length = analysis_params["current"].get("sliding_window_length")
        if sliding_window_length is None:
            sliding_window_length = analysis_params["default"].get(
                "sliding_window_length", 0.5
            )

        window_length_ratio = analysis_params["current"].get("window_length_ratio")
        if window_length_ratio in (None, 0):
            window_length_ratio = analysis_params["default"].get(
                "window_length_ratio", 5
            )

        short_window_length = sliding_window_length / window_length_ratio
        step_length = short_window_length  # step matches short window length

        k_weight = analysis_params["current"].get("crash_weights")["K"]
        if k_weight in (None, 0):
            k_weight = analysis_params["default"].get("crash_weights")["K"]
        a_weight = analysis_params["current"].get("crash_weights")["A"]
        if a_weight in (None, 0):
            a_weight = analysis_params["default"].get("crash_weights")["A"]
        b_weight = analysis_params["current"].get("crash_weights")["B"]
        if b_weight in (None, 0):
            b_weight = analysis_params["default"].get("crash_weights")["B"]
        c_weight = analysis_params["current"].get("crash_weights")["C"]
        if c_weight in (None, 0):
            c_weight = analysis_params["default"].get("crash_weights")["C"]
        o_weight = analysis_params["current"].get("crash_weights")["O"]
        if o_weight in (None, 0):
            o_weight = analysis_params["default"].get("crash_weights")["O"]

        SLIDING_WINDOW_RESULTS = analysis_utils.get_sliding_window_results(study_id)
        _SWA_RAN = True


def plot_top_corridors_map(mode):
    if not _SWA_RAN:
        return  # section-level message already displayed in setup cell
    gdf = TOP_CORRIDOR_DICT.get(mode)
    if gdf is None or gdf.empty:
        display(Markdown("_No map data available for this mode._"))
        return
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=MAP_FIG_SIZE)
    map_utils.plot_top_corridor_map(
        ax=ax,
        corridors_gdf=gdf,
        study_area_gdf=STUDY_AREA_GDF,
    )
    plt.show()


def plot_sliding_windows_map(mode):
    if not _SWA_RAN:
        return  # section-level message already displayed in setup cell
    gdf = SLIDING_WINDOW_RESULTS
    show_col = f"show_{mode}"
    if gdf is None or gdf.empty or not gdf[show_col].any():
        display(Markdown("_No map data available for this mode._"))
        return
    gdf = gdf[gdf[show_col]]
    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=MAP_FIG_SIZE)
    map_utils.plot_sliding_window_map(
        ax=ax,
        mode=mode,
        segments_gdf=gdf,
        study_area_gdf=STUDY_AREA_GDF,
    )
    plt.show()


def plot_top_corridors_table(mode):
    if not _SWA_RAN:
        return  # section-level message already displayed in setup cell
    gdf = TOP_CORRIDOR_DICT.get(mode)
    if gdf is None or gdf.empty:
        print("No data available.")
        return
    table_df = gdf[["road_name", "functional_class", "crash_score"]].copy()
    table_df.columns = ["Corridor Name", "Functional Class", "Crash Score"]
    table_df["Crash Score"] = table_df["Crash Score"].round(2)
    display(Markdown(table_df.to_markdown(index=False)))
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: true
if _SWA_RAN:
    display(Markdown(
        f"## Method\n\n"
        f"This analysis takes the crashes and roads data within the study area and allocates the crashes to roads, "
        f"measured on {sliding_window_length}- mile sliding window segments stepped in {short_window_length}-mile increments along the network. "
        f"The sliding windows score weights the most severe crashes more heavily than lower severity crashes. "
        f"The Sliding Windows Score is calculated by multiplying the number of Fatal (K) crashes by {k_weight}, "
        f"Incapacitating Injury (A) crashes by {a_weight}, Non-Incapacitating Injury (B) crashes by {b_weight}, "
        f"Possible Injury (C) crashes by {c_weight}, and Property Damage Only (O) crashes by {o_weight}. "
        f"Once the weights are established and applied to the crashes, the total number of crashes are aggregated "
        f"along a corridor while incorporating the crash severity weighting. "
        f"If you used FARS data alone, only fatal crashes will have been used."
    ))
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: true
if _SWA_RAN:
    display(Markdown(
        f"## Results Visualization\n\n"
        f"The table and maps below highlight the top {top_corridor_limit} crash corridors for each mode "
        f"as measured by the total overall Sliding Windows Scores for each sliding window segment."
    ))

    _MODE_LABELS = {"ped": "Pedestrian", "bike": "Bicycle", "mv": "Motor Vehicle"}

    for mode, display_name in _MODE_LABELS.items():
        display(Markdown("{{< pagebreak >}}"))
        display(Markdown(f"### {display_name} Sliding Windows Analysis\n\n"))
        plot_sliding_windows_map(mode)
        display(Markdown("{{< pagebreak >}}"))
        display(Markdown(f"#### Top {top_corridor_limit} {display_name} Crash Corridors"))
        plot_top_corridors_map(mode)
        plot_top_corridors_table(mode)
```
